zh.lifeSummary: 當Cornelius Diffee於1790年出生在美國北卡羅來納州蘭多夫時，他的父親John Diffee Sr26歲，母親Sarah Jane Swafford21歲。他於大約1814年與Sarah Cagle在美國田納西州林肯結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1850年住在美國阿肯色州卡羅爾達爾 堂史普。他去世於1850年在美國阿肯色州克拉克享年60歲。

nameMeanings.name: Diffee
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Diffey  . Compare   Defee  .

History: William Diffee, from whom present-day American bearers of this name are descended, was born c. 1710, probably in Ireland, and died in 1790 in Randolph County, NC. His surname was also spelled Diffey, while his descendants also bear the surname Defee .

variants.name: Diffey
meanings: English (Dorset): perhaps a nickname from Anglo-Norman French defaé, defieu ‘faithless, disloyal’. Compare Diffee .

variants.name: Defee
meanings: History: This surname is borne by the descendants of William Diffee, born c. 1710, probably in Ireland, died 1790 in Randolph County, NC. The same spelling is recorded in London, England, in the 18th century.

nameMeanings.name: Cornelius
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an old Roman family name, Cornélius, which is of uncertain origin, possibly a derivative of Latin cornu ‘horn’. This was the name of a 3rd-century pope who is venerated as a saint. The name was particularly popular in the Low Countries, and immigrants contributed to its frequency in the north of England from the 1400s. However, it is now seldom used in Britain.

historicEvents.description: Bill of Rights guarantees individual freedom.

historicEvents.title: Capital City Moved
historicEvents.description: New Bern had been the capital of North Carolina in its early days. In 1792, the capital was moved to Raleigh because it was more the center of the state.
historicEvents.urlTitle: National Park Service- Raleigh: A Capital City

historicEvents.description: Historical Boundaries 1819: Clark, Missouri Territory, United States 1819: Clark, Arkansas Territory, United States 1836: Clark, Arkansas, United States

historicEvents.title: Financial Relief for Public Land
historicEvents.description: A United States law to provide financial relief for the purchasers of Public Lands. It permitted the earlier buyers, that couldn't pay completely for the land, to return the land back to the government. This granted them a credit towards the debt they had on land. Congress, also, extended credit to buyer for eight more years. Still while being in economic panic and the shortage of currency made by citizens, the government hoped that with the time extension, the economy would improve.

historicEvents.title: Trail of Tears
historicEvents.description: In the 1830's, President Jackson called for all the Native Americans to be forced off their own land. As the Cherokee were forced out of North Carolina many of them hid in the mountains of North Carolina.
historicEvents.urlTitle: North Carolina Trail of Tears Association

historicEvents.title: Remember the Alamo
historicEvents.description: Being a monumental event in the Texas Revolution, The Battle of the Alamo was a thirteen-day battle at the Alamo Mission near San Antonio. In the early morning of the final battle, the Mexican Army advanced on the Alamo. Quickly being overrun, the Texian Soldiers quickly withdrew inside the building. The battle has often been overshadowed by events from the Mexican–American War, But the Alamo gradually became known as a national battle site and later named an official Texas State Shrine.
U.S. acquires vast tracts of Mexican territory in wake of Mexican War including California and New Mexico.

en.lifeSummary: When Cornelius Diffee was born in 1790, in Randolph, North Carolina, United States, his father, John Diffee Sr, was 26 and his mother, Sarah Jane Swafford, was 21. He married Sarah Cagle about 1814, in Lincoln, Tennessee, United States. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He lived in Cedar Township, Carroll, Arkansas, United States in 1850. He died in 1850, in Clark, Arkansas, United States, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Cornelius Diffee est né en 1790, à Randolph, Caroline du Nord, États-Unis, son père, John Diffee Sr, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Sarah Jane Swafford, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Sarah Cagle environ 1814, à Lincoln, Tennessee, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il a vécu à Cedar Township, Carroll, Arkansas, États-Unis en 1850. Il est décédé en 1850, à Clark, Arkansas, États-Unis, à l’âge de 60 ans.

Er hat um 1814 in Lincoln, Tennessee, Vereinigte Staaten Sarah Cagle geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. 1850 hat er in Cedar Township, Carroll, Arkansas, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt. Er starb 1850 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Clark, Arkansas, Vereinigte Staaten.

ja.lifeSummary: Cornelius Diffeeが1790年にアメリカ合衆国ノースカロライナ州ランドルフで生まれた時，父John Diffee Srは26歳，母Sarah Jane Swaffordは21歳であった。約1814年にアメリカ合衆国テネシー州リンコルンでSarah Cagleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1850年にはアメリカ合衆国アーカンソー州カーロルセダー トウンシュイップに住んでいた。彼は1850年にアメリカ合衆国アーカンソー州クラークで60歳で死去した。

ko.Cedar, Clark, Arkansas, United States: 미국 아칸소 주 카로 세더르 타운시프
ko.lifeSummary: Cornelius Diffee(이)가 1790년 미국 노스캐롤라이나 주 랜돌프에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Diffee Sr(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Sarah Jane Swafford(은)는 21세였다. 그는 대략 1814년 미국 테네시 주 링코컨에서 Sarah Cagle(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1850년도에 미국 아칸소 주 카로 세더르 타운시프에서 살았다. 그는 1850년 미국 아칸소 주 클라르크에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Cedar, Clark, Arkansas, United States: кедар тоуншип, карролл, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Cornelius Diffee родился в 1790 г. в местности рандолф, Северная Каролина, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени John Diffee Sr было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Sarah Jane Swafford, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Sarah Cagle около 1814 г. в местности линколн, Теннесси, Соединенные штаты. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он жил в местности кедар тоуншип, карролл, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты в 1850 году. Он умер в 1850 г. в местности кларк, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 60 лет.
